qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Admin API endpoints lack authentication allowing unauthorized worker restart and information disclosure
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes session data leakage caused by frankenphp_reset_super_globals failing to delete $_SESSION between requests. This finding describes missing authentication on admin API endpoints in caddy/admin.go. The root cause, vulnerable code, and vulnerability class are entirely different.

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Path traversal in worker path matching allows unauthorized PHP script execution
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes session data leakage from frankenphp_reset_super_globals not resetting $_SESSION. This finding describes unsafe string concatenation in workerconfig.go:181 allowing path traversal in worker script matching. Different vulnerable code, different root cause, different vulnerability class.

target_advisory_judgement.reason: Neither qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerability: FrankenPHP's failure to reset $_SESSION between requests in worker mode (frankenphp_reset_super_globals). Finding admin-api-no-auth describes missing auth on admin API endpoints (different root cause). Finding worker-path-traversal describes unsafe string concatenation in workerconfig.go path matching (different root cause).
evidence: Advisory root cause: frankenphp_reset_super_globals does not explicitly delete $_SESSION from the Zend Engine symbol table between worker-mode requests
admin-api-no-auth targets caddy/admin.go handler functions for /frankenphp/workers/restart and /frankenphp/threads — unrelated to session handling
worker-path-traversal targets workerconfig.go:181 string concatenation in matchesPath() — unrelated to session superglobal reset
